examples: Trimethylamine is the chemical that produces the 'fishy' smell we all recognise.
Shredded squid as a popular snack food is rich in trimethylamine-n-oxide (TMAO).
Synthesis of cationic lignin flocculant by lignin, epichlorohydrin and trimethylamine was obtained.
B-complex vitamin found in fish, meat, soyabeans, eggs and other foods, which is changed into trimethylamine and so accumulates in people who cannot deodorise the molecule.
